---
title: "Scatter Plot Widget Roadmap"
format: html
---

# ScatterPlotWidget Development Roadmap

This document tracks remaining development tasks for the Scatter Plot Widget. For documentation
of completed features and current architecture, see the [main developer documentation](index.qmd).

## Completed Work Summary

The following features are complete and documented in [index.qmd](index.qmd):

- **Widget skeleton**: EditorState pattern, registration, state/view/properties factories
- **State management**: `ScatterPlotState` with `HorizontalAxisState`, `VerticalAxisState` composition; `ScatterPlotStateData` serializable struct
- **Axis system**: `HorizontalAxisWidget` (X) and `VerticalAxisWidget` (Y) with bidirectional sync, including the "silent update" pattern
- **Interaction**: Separated X/Y zoom, pan via shared `PlotInteractionHelpers`
- **Properties panel**: Axis range controls in collapsible `Section` widgets; data source selection UI (key, column, offset per axis); reference line toggle; compatibility status label
- **OpenGL lifecycle**: `ScatterPlotOpenGLWidget` initialization, projection/view matrix management, pan/zoom mouse handlers
- **State serialization**: Full round-trip JSON via `rfl::json`
- **Registration wiring**: `timePositionSelected` → `EditorRegistry::setCurrentTime()` connection
- **Data source descriptor**: `ScatterAxisSource` struct defining data key, tensor column, and temporal offset
- **Row type compatibility validation**: `resolveSourceRowType()` and `checkSourceCompatibility()` functions in `Core/SourceCompatibility.hpp/.cpp` with full test coverage (21 test cases, 52 assertions)
- **Data source UI**: Key/column/offset combo boxes per axis with DataManager observer, compatibility label displaying `checkSourceCompatibility()` results
- **Point pair computation**: `buildScatterPoints()` factory function in `Core/BuildScatterPoints.hpp/.cpp` supporting all compatible source type combinations (ATS×ATS, ATS×TensorTFI, TensorTFI×ATS, TensorTFI×TensorTFI, Ordinal×Ordinal, Interval×Interval) with temporal offset and TimeFrameIndex join. Tested in `BuildScatterPoints.test.cpp`.
- **Point rendering**: SceneBuilder→SceneRenderer pipeline using `addGlyphs()` for points with single instanced draw call
- **Auto-fit bounds**: Automatic X/Y bounds detection from data with 5% padding on first data load (when bounds are at defaults)
- **Reference line rendering**: $y = x$ diagonal via `addPolyLine()` with show/hide toggle in properties panel; endpoints update on pan/zoom
- **DataManager observer**: Observer registration/removal in properties widget for combo box synchronization; stale key handling clears axis source when selected data key is deleted from DataManager; tested in `ScatterPlotPropertiesWidget.test.cpp`
- **Double-click-to-navigate**: Point hit testing via shared `SceneHitTester` + `QuadTree<EntityId>` spatial index, `pointDoubleClicked` → `timePositionSelected` → `EditorRegistry::setCurrentTime()` signal chain, yellow highlight glyph for navigated-to point
- **Glyph customization**: `GlyphStyleData` in `ScatterPlotStateData` with default blue circle style (`#3388FF`, size 5, alpha 0.8); `GlyphStyleState` owned by `ScatterPlotState` with `glyphStyleChanged` signal; `GlyphStyleControls` embedded in properties panel "Glyph Options" collapsible section; rendering uses `toRenderableGlyphType()` and `hexColorToVec4()` from `GlyphStyleConversion.hpp`; full round-trip JSON serialization
- **Point selection**: Dual selection modes (SinglePoint/Polygon) via combo box in properties panel; SinglePoint mode: Ctrl+Click toggles selection, Shift+Click removes; Polygon mode: Ctrl+Click adds vertices, Enter closes polygon, Backspace undoes, Escape cancels; `selectPointsInPolygon()` utility in `CorePlotting/Selection/`; selected points rendered with distinct style (orange, +4 size); integration with `GroupContextMenuHandler` for right-click group operations; fully tested
- **Temporal offset for outlier detection**: Per-axis `time_offset` field in `ScatterAxisSource` enables lag-plots ($x(t)$ vs $x(t-1)$); `buildScatterPoints()` applies offsets for all source type combinations; offset spinboxes (-10000 to 10000) in properties panel per axis; points where offset pushes index outside valid range are excluded; tested in `BuildScatterPoints.test.cpp`

- **Hover tooltip**: `PlotTooltipManager` integration showing X/Y values and `TimeFrameIndex` on dwell; pan/zoom suppression; text provider with 3-decimal precision
- **ContextAction integration**: Right-click context menu dynamically populates applicable ContextActions from `SelectionContext`; `setSelectionContext()` wired in registration; enables cross-widget ML workflows
- **Selective clustering ("Cluster Selection...")**: Context menu action extracts selected point rows from source `TensorData`, creates a filtered `TensorData` stored as `"<key>_selection"`, sets data focus, and programmatically triggers the `mlcore.cluster_tensor` ContextAction
- **Cluster label overlay**: QPainter text overlay showing group name + count at per-group centroids over the OpenGL scatter; `show_cluster_labels` state field with checkbox in properties panel

- **Feature color shared library** (`CorePlotting/FeatureColor/`): `FeatureColorSourceDescriptor`, `resolveFeatureColors()`, `checkFeatureColorCompatibility()`, `applyFeatureColorsToScene()`, `computeEffectiveColorRange()` — all implemented and tested (24 test cases, 74 assertions). See [FeatureColor module docs](../../../coreplotting/feature_color.qmd).

**Not yet implemented**:
feature coloring widget integration (Phase 7.2–7.9), stale key handling (Phase 6.3).

---

## Phase 7: External Feature Coloring

**Status**: Phase 7.1 (shared `CorePlotting/FeatureColor/` library) ✅ complete. Phases 7.2–7.9 (widget integration) ready for implementation.

**Goal**: Color scatter points by an external data source, enabling visual analysis of relationships
between the plotted X/Y variables and other measured quantities. Three concrete scenarios motivate
the design:

1. **Categorical overlay**: TensorData X/Y (TimeFrameIndex rows) colored by whether each point's
   `TimeFrameIndex` falls inside a `DigitalIntervalSeries` (e.g., highlight licking bouts).
2. **Continuous heatmap (interval rows)**: TensorData X/Y (Interval rows) colored by a continuous
   value from another TensorData column with matching Interval rows (e.g., trial duration).
3. **Continuous heatmap (timeframe rows)**: AnalogTimeSeries X/Y colored by a second
   AnalogTimeSeries at each `TimeFrameIndex` (e.g., velocity colored by acceleration).

### 7.0 Architecture Overview

Feature coloring is implemented as a **post-`buildScatterPoints()` color override** — the same
position where `applyGroupColorsToScene()` currently operates. The pipeline is:

```
buildScatterPoints()          ← produces ScatterPointData with time_indices
    → addGlyphs()             ← uniform color from GlyphStyleData
    → applyFeatureColorsToScene()  ← NEW: per-point color override via RenderableGlyphBatch::colors[]
    → applyGroupColorsToScene()    ← existing: group colors override feature colors for grouped points
    → uploadScene()
```

This approach requires **no changes** to `SceneBuilder`, `GlyphRenderer`, or shader code — the
per-instance `colors` VBO (`a_instance_color`, location 2) is already populated per-element by
`addGlyphs()` and directly mutated before upload. Group colors take priority over feature colors,
matching existing behavior where group coloring overrides the default glyph color.

### 7.1 Shared Core: `CorePlotting/FeatureColor/` ✅ Complete

**Implementation**: `src/CorePlotting/FeatureColor/` — see the [FeatureColor module docs](../../../coreplotting/feature_color.qmd) for full API reference.

The color source resolution logic is shared across widgets (ScatterPlotWidget, EventPlotWidget,
LinePlotWidget) since all face the same problem: "given a set of time-anchored points, look up
a color value from an external data object." All files are built as part of the `CorePlotting`
static library.

#### 7.1.1 Color Source Types

Each `ScatterPointData` point has a `TimeFrameIndex` from `time_indices[i]`. The color source
must resolve that index to a `float` (for continuous colormapping) or a `float` in {0, 1} (for
binary coloring). Three source types cover all current data objects:

| Color Source Type | Data Object | Lookup Method | Output |
|-------------------|-------------|---------------|--------|
| **AnalogTimeSeries** | `AnalogTimeSeries` | `getAtTime(tfi)` → `std::optional<float>` | Continuous float |
| **TensorData column** | `TensorData` | Join on matching `TimeFrameIndex` or positional match for Interval/Ordinal | Continuous float |
| **DigitalIntervalSeries** | `DigitalIntervalSeries` | `hasIntervalAtTime(tfi, tf)` → bool (1.0/0.0) | Binary float |

#### 7.1.2 `FeatureColorSourceDescriptor` — `CorePlotting/FeatureColor/FeatureColorSourceDescriptor.hpp`

Serializable descriptor pointing to a DataManager key and, optionally, a TensorData column by
name (`tensor_column_name`) or index (`tensor_column_index`).

#### 7.1.3 `resolveFeatureColors()` — `CorePlotting/FeatureColor/ResolveFeatureColors.hpp/.cpp`

Returns a `vector<optional<float>>` parallel to `point_times`. Join strategy is selected
automatically based on the color source's data type:

| Color Source Object | Join Strategy |
|---------------------|---------------|
| `AnalogTimeSeries` | `getAtTime(point_time)` per point |
| `TensorData` (TimeFrameIndex rows) | Hash-join: unordered_map of TFI → row, then column lookup |
| `TensorData` (Ordinal rows) | Positional: row *i* → point *i* |
| `DigitalIntervalSeries` | `hasIntervalAtTime(point_time, tf)` → 1.0 / 0.0 |

Points where the lookup fails produce `nullopt` and retain their default glyph color.

#### 7.1.4 `applyFeatureColorsToScene()` — `CorePlotting/FeatureColor/ApplyFeatureColors.hpp/.cpp`

Mutates `RenderableGlyphBatch::colors[]` for all glyph batches whose `entity_id` values fall
within the scatter point range. Points in `skip_indices` or with `nullopt` resolved values
retain their existing color. Dispatches on `FeatureColorMapping` (`ContinuousMapping` or
`ThresholdMapping`) via `std::visit`.

- **`ContinuousMapping`** (`FeatureColorMapping.hpp`): colormap preset + vmin/vmax normalization via `Colormaps::mapValue()`.
- **`ThresholdMapping`** (`FeatureColorMapping.hpp`): value ≥ threshold → `above_color`, else `below_color`.

Both strategies share the same `resolveFeatureColors()` pipeline; only the float→color step differs.
When the user selects a `DigitalIntervalSeries` key, the UI should auto-default to threshold
mode (threshold=0.5) — but can be overridden to continuous if a gradient view is preferred.

### 7.2 Compatibility Validation ✅ Complete

**Implementation**: `CorePlotting/FeatureColor/FeatureColorCompatibility.hpp/.cpp`

Not all color source / scatter source combinations are meaningful. Validation happens when the
user selects a color source. `checkFeatureColorCompatibility()` returns a
`FeatureColorCompatibilityResult{bool compatible, string reason}` and validates:

- The DataManager key exists and resolves to a supported type (ATS, TensorData, or DIS)
- For TensorData: the specified column name or index exists
- Row types are compatible per the table below

| Scatter Row Type | Valid Color Sources | Join Strategy |
|------------------|---------------------|---------------|
| `AnalogTimeSeries` | ATS, TensorData(TFI), DigitalIntervalSeries | TimeFrameIndex lookup |
| `TensorTimeFrameIndex` | ATS, TensorData(TFI), DigitalIntervalSeries | TimeFrameIndex lookup |
| `TensorInterval` | TensorData(Interval) with same row count | Positional match |
| `TensorOrdinal` | TensorData(Ordinal) with same row count | Positional match |

### 7.3 State Changes: `ScatterColorConfigData`

Add a new serializable struct to `ScatterPlotStateData`:

```cpp
/// @brief Configuration for external feature-based coloring.
///
/// Lives in: ScatterPlotWidget/Core/ScatterColorConfig.hpp
struct ScatterColorConfigData {
    std::string color_mode = "fixed";              ///< "fixed" or "by_feature"

    // Feature color source (only used when color_mode == "by_feature")
    std::optional<FeatureColorSourceDescriptor> color_source;

    // How resolved float values become colors
    std::string mapping_mode = "continuous";        ///< "continuous" or "threshold"

    // -- Continuous mode settings --
    std::string colormap_preset = "viridis";       ///< Colormap name (matches ColormapPreset)
    ColorRangeConfig color_range;                   ///< Auto/Manual/Symmetric range control

    // -- Threshold mode settings --
    double threshold = 0.5;                         ///< Binarization threshold
    std::string above_color = "#FF4444";            ///< Hex color for values >= threshold
    std::string below_color = "#3388FF";            ///< Hex color for values < threshold
    float above_alpha = 0.9f;                       ///< Alpha for above-threshold points
    float below_alpha = 0.9f;                       ///< Alpha for below-threshold points
};
```

The `mapping_mode` field selects between the two rendering strategies:

| `mapping_mode` | Input | Output | UI Controls |
|----------------|-------|--------|-------------|
| `"continuous"` | float → colormap($t$) | Gradient color | Colormap preset, range (Auto/Manual/Symmetric) |
| `"threshold"` | float $\ge$ threshold? | Binary two-color | Threshold spinbox, above/below color pickers |

Add to `ScatterPlotStateData`:

```cpp
struct ScatterPlotStateData {
    // ... existing fields ...
    ScatterColorConfigData color_config;  ///< NEW: feature coloring configuration
};
```

Add to `ScatterPlotState`:

```cpp
class ScatterPlotState : public EditorState {
    // ... existing ...
signals:
    void colorConfigChanged();  ///< NEW: emitted when color_config changes

public:
    [[nodiscard]] ScatterColorConfigData const & colorConfig() const;
    void setColorConfig(ScatterColorConfigData config);
};
```

The `colorConfigChanged` signal connects to `_scene_dirty = true` in
`ScatterPlotOpenGLWidget::setState()`, triggering a `rebuildScene()`.

### 7.4 Auto-Range Computation ✅ Complete

**Implementation**: `CorePlotting/FeatureColor/FeatureColorRange.hpp/.cpp`

When using **continuous** mapping mode and `ColorRangeConfig::mode == Auto`, the color range
is computed from the resolved feature values via `computeEffectiveColorRange()`. `Symmetric`
mode yields `[-max_abs, +max_abs]`. Returns `optional<pair<float,float>>` — `nullopt` when
no valid values exist (e.g. all `nullopt`) in Auto or Symmetric mode. Not used by
`ThresholdMapping`.

Supported `ColorRangeMode` values: `Auto`, `Manual` (user-specified vmin/vmax), `Symmetric`.

### 7.5 Rendering Integration

In `ScatterPlotOpenGLWidget::rebuildScene()`, after `builder.build()` and before
`applyGroupColorsToScene()`:

```cpp
auto scene = builder.build();
_scene = std::make_unique<CorePlotting::RenderableScene>(std::move(scene));

// NEW: Apply feature coloring (before group colors, which take priority)
auto const & cc = _state->colorConfig();
if (cc.color_mode == "by_feature" && cc.color_source.has_value()) {
    auto const feature_values = resolveFeatureColors(
        *_data_manager, *cc.color_source,
        _scatter_data.time_indices, _scatter_data.source_row_type);

    FeatureColorMapping mapping;
    if (cc.mapping_mode == "threshold") {
        mapping = ThresholdMapping{
            static_cast<float>(cc.threshold),
            hexColorToVec4(cc.above_color, cc.above_alpha),
            hexColorToVec4(cc.below_color, cc.below_alpha)};
    } else {
        auto const preset = CorePlotting::colormapPresetFromString(cc.colormap_preset);
        auto const [vmin, vmax] = computeEffectiveColorRange(feature_values, cc.color_range);
        mapping = ContinuousMapping{preset, vmin, vmax};
    }

    applyFeatureColorsToScene(*_scene, "scatter_points", feature_values, mapping);
}

applyGroupColorsToScene();  // Existing: group colors override feature colors
_scene_renderer->uploadScene(*_scene);
```

**Color priority** (highest to lowest, unchanged from existing design):

1. **Navigated highlight** (yellow) — `scatter_highlight` batch
2. **Selected** (orange) — `scatter_selected` batch
3. **Group color** — `applyGroupColorsToScene()` overrides per-point
4. **Feature color** — NEW: `applyFeatureColorsToScene()` overrides default
5. **Default glyph style** — `GlyphStyleData` hex color

### 7.6 Properties Panel: "Point Coloring" Section

Add a collapsible `Section` to `ScatterPlotPropertiesWidget` between "Glyph Options" and
"Selection Mode":

```
┌─ Point Coloring ─────────────────────────────┐
│  Color Mode:  [ Fixed ▾ ]                    │  ← QComboBox: "Fixed" / "By Feature"
│                                               │
│  ── (visible only when "By Feature") ──       │
│  Data Key:    [ velocity ▾ ]                  │  ← QComboBox: all DM keys (ATS, TensorData, DIS)
│  Column:      [ col_0 ▾ ]                     │  ← QComboBox: columns (TensorData only, hidden for ATS/DIS)
│  Status:      ✓ Compatible (ATS→ATS join)     │  ← QLabel: compatibility result
│                                               │
│  Mapping:     [ Continuous ▾ ]                │  ← QComboBox: "Continuous" / "Threshold"
│                                               │
│  ── (visible when Continuous) ───────────     │
│  ┌─ Colormap ──────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  [ColormapControls widget]              │  │  ← Reuse existing ColormapControls
│  │  Preset: [ Viridis ▾ ]                  │  │
│  │  Range:  [ Auto ▾ ]  Min: [___] Max:[___]│ │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                               │
│  ── (visible when Threshold) ────────────     │
│  Threshold:   [ 0.500    ]                    │  ← QDoubleSpinBox
│  Above color: [■ #FF4444 ]                    │  ← Color button (opens QColorDialog)
│  Below color: [■ #3388FF ]                    │  ← Color button (opens QColorDialog)
└───────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

When a `DigitalIntervalSeries` is selected as the data key, the UI auto-switches to
Threshold mode with threshold=0.5 as sensible defaults (since DIS resolves to {0.0, 1.0}),
but the user can override this to Continuous if they prefer a colormap view.

**Data Key combo box** population: Lists all DataManager keys whose type is one of:

- `AnalogTimeSeries` — always shown (compatible with TFI-based scatter sources)
- `TensorData` — always shown (compatibility checked dynamically)
- `DigitalIntervalSeries` — always shown (binary coloring for TFI-based scatter sources)

The Column combo box is shown only when a `TensorData` key is selected. For
`AnalogTimeSeries` and `DigitalIntervalSeries`, it is hidden since those types have a single
implicit value dimension.

**Compatibility label** updates dynamically via `checkFeatureColorCompatibility()` whenever
the user changes the data key or column selection.

### 7.7 Tooltip Enhancement

When feature coloring is active, the hover tooltip (via `PlotTooltipManager`) should include
the feature value in addition to X, Y, and TimeFrameIndex:

```
X: 1.234  Y: 5.678
TFI: 42
Color: 0.891 (velocity)           ← continuous mode
```

or for threshold mode:

```
X: 1.234  Y: 5.678
TFI: 42
Color: 25.3 (velocity) [above]    ← threshold mode, value >= threshold
```

This requires extending the text provider callback to query the cached feature values.

### 7.8 Implementation Sub-Steps

| Step | Component | Location | Status |
|------|-----------|----------|---------|
| **7.8.1** | `FeatureColorSourceDescriptor` | `CorePlotting/FeatureColor/` | ✅ Done |
| **7.8.2** | `resolveFeatureColors()` | `CorePlotting/FeatureColor/` | ✅ Done |
| **7.8.3** | `checkFeatureColorCompatibility()` | `CorePlotting/FeatureColor/` | ✅ Done |
| **7.8.4** | `applyFeatureColorsToScene()` | `CorePlotting/FeatureColor/` | ✅ Done |
| **7.8.5** | `computeEffectiveColorRange()` | `CorePlotting/FeatureColor/` | ✅ Done |
| **7.8.6** | `ScatterColorConfigData` | `ScatterPlotWidget/Core/` | ⏳ Not started |
| **7.8.7** | Properties panel UI | `ScatterPlotWidget/UI/` | ⏳ Not started |
| **7.8.8** | Rendering integration | `ScatterPlotWidget/Rendering/` | ⏳ Not started |
| **7.8.9** | Tooltip extension | `ScatterPlotWidget/Rendering/` | ⏳ Not started |
| **7.8.10** | Unit tests | `tests/ScatterPlotWidget/` | ⏳ Not started |

Steps 7.8.1–7.8.5 (shared `CorePlotting/FeatureColor/` infrastructure) are complete — 24
test cases, 74 assertions in `tests/CorePlotting/FeatureColor.test.cpp`. See
[FeatureColor module docs](../../../coreplotting/feature_color.qmd).
Steps 7.8.6–7.8.9 are ScatterPlotWidget-specific and remain to be implemented.

### 7.9 Shared Infrastructure: Cross-Widget Usage

The `CorePlotting/FeatureColor/` library is designed for reuse across plot widgets:

| Widget | How It Uses the Shared Library |
|--------|-------------------------------|
| **ScatterPlotWidget** | `resolveFeatureColors()` with `time_indices` from `ScatterPointData` |
| **[EventPlotWidget](../EventPlotWidget/roadmap.qmd)** | `resolveFeatureColors()` with event `TimeFrameIndex` values per trial; colors events in raster rows |
| **[LinePlotWidget](../LinePlotWidget/roadmap.qmd)** | `resolveFeatureColors()` with trial interval start times; colors whole lines per trial |

All three widgets share:

- `FeatureColorSourceDescriptor` for serialization
- `resolveFeatureColors()` for the lookup
- `applyFeatureColorsToScene()` for the color override
- `ColormapControls` for the UI
- `checkFeatureColorCompatibility()` for validation

Each widget only needs to provide its own `time_indices` and integrate the color config
into its own `EditorState`.

### 7.10 Design Decisions

7. **Post-build color override (not per-point GlyphStyle)**: Feature colors are applied by
   mutating `RenderableGlyphBatch::colors[]` after `addGlyphs()` fills uniform colors. This
   avoids changing `SceneBuilder` or `GlyphRenderer` APIs and follows the same pattern as
   `applyGroupColorsToScene()`. The tradeoff is two passes over the color array (feature
   colors, then group colors), but this is negligible vs. the GPU draw call.

8. **Threshold mode as generalized binary coloring**: Rather than treating
   `DigitalIntervalSeries` as a special case, the design introduces a general **threshold
   mapping mode** that binarizes any float value at a user-defined cutoff. This naturally
   handles three cases with one mechanism:
   - `DigitalIntervalSeries` (already 0/1, threshold=0.5)
   - TensorData columns with binary labels (0/1 classifier output, threshold=0.5)
   - Continuous data with a meaningful cutoff ("highlight velocity > 20", threshold=20.0)

   The threshold mode is a peer of continuous colormap mode, not a special case of it.
   Both share `resolveFeatureColors()` for the lookup; only the float→color step differs
   (`ContinuousMapping` vs `ThresholdMapping` in the `FeatureColorMapping` variant).
   When the user selects a `DigitalIntervalSeries` key, the UI auto-defaults to threshold
   mode — but the user can always switch to continuous if they prefer a gradient view.

9. **Shared `CorePlotting/FeatureColor/` library**: The resolution logic is identical across
   widgets — only the source of `time_indices` differs. Factoring into `CorePlotting` avoids
   duplicating join logic in each widget and ensures consistent behavior.

10. **`ScatterSourceRowType` reuse**: The `resolveFeatureColors()` function takes
    `ScatterSourceRowType` to determine the join strategy. When EventPlotWidget or
    LinePlotWidget call it, they pass the equivalent row type for their data (always
    `TensorInterval` for trial-aligned widgets). The enum may be renamed to a more generic
    `PlotSourceRowType` during implementation if warranted.

---

## Shared Components Summary

Components that have been factored into shared locations or are candidates for sharing:

| Component | Status | Location | Used By |
|-----------|--------|----------|---------|
| `selectPointsInPolygon` | ✅ **Implemented** | `CorePlotting/Selection/` | ScatterPlotWidget; available for [TemporalProjectionViewWidget](../TemporalProjectionViewWidget/index.qmd) |
| `SelectionInstructions` | ✅ **Implemented** | `Plots/Common/SelectionInstructions.hpp` | ScatterPlotWidget, TemporalProjectionViewWidget |
| `GroupManagementPanel` | ⏸️ **Deferred** | N/A | ScatterPlotWidget uses existing `GroupContextMenuHandler` via right-click; dedicated panel not needed yet |
| `Colormaps` | ✅ **Implemented** | `CorePlotting/Colormaps/` | HeatmapWidget; planned for ScatterPlotWidget (Phase 7), [EventPlotWidget](../EventPlotWidget/roadmap.qmd), [LinePlotWidget](../LinePlotWidget/roadmap.qmd) |
| `ColormapControls` | ✅ **Implemented** | `Plots/Common/ColormapControls/` | HeatmapWidget; planned for ScatterPlotWidget (Phase 7), EventPlotWidget, LinePlotWidget |
| `FeatureColorSource` | ✅ **Implemented** | `CorePlotting/FeatureColor/` | ScatterPlotWidget (Phase 7), [EventPlotWidget](../EventPlotWidget/roadmap.qmd) (Phase 5), [LinePlotWidget](../LinePlotWidget/roadmap.qmd) (Phase 3) — shared resolution, compatibility, and scene override logic. 24 tests, 74 assertions. |


---

## Phase 8: Hover Tooltip

**Status**: ✅ **Implemented**. See [index.qmd](index.qmd) § Hover Tooltip for documentation.

---

## Design Decisions

1. **Point count scalability**: The `SceneBuilder::addGlyphs()` → `GlyphRenderer` pipeline uses
   instanced rendering (single GPU draw call for all points in a batch). This is the same path
   EventPlotWidget uses for large spike counts and scales well to tens of thousands of points.
   No dedicated `PointBatchRenderer` is needed.

2. **Polygon interaction**: Uses the existing `PolygonInteractionController` from
   `CorePlotting/Interaction/`. This shared controller handles click-based polygon creation,
   vertex drag modification, cursor preview, and auto-close detection.

3. **Reference line**: Only $y = x$ reference line for now. Can be generalized to
   arbitrary linear reference lines ($y = ax + b$) in a future phase if needed.

4. **Hit testing via shared QuadTree**: Point hit testing uses the existing `SceneHitTester`
   (`CorePlotting/Interaction/SceneHitTester.hpp`) which queries the `QuadTree<EntityId>`
   spatial index built automatically by `SceneBuilder::addGlyphs()`. This is the same
   infrastructure used by [EventPlotWidget](../EventPlotWidget/index.qmd) for double-click
   navigation and click-to-select. The QuadTree provides `O(log n)` nearest-neighbor lookup,
   scaling well to large point counts without widget-specific spatial indexing code. Navigation
   uses `mouseDoubleClickEvent`, matching EventPlotWidget. Hover tooltips (Phase 8) use the
   shared [PlotTooltipManager](../Common/TooltipManager/index.qmd) for dwell timer and
   lifecycle management.

5. **Two-batch rendering for selection/highlight**: `GlyphRenderer` renders each `RenderableGlyphBatch` in order, with its own `u_point_size` / `u_glyph_size` uniform. Per-instance color is already supported by the shader (`a_instance_color` / `a_color` VBO attribute), but per-instance size is not — size is a per-batch uniform. Therefore, rendering selected/highlighted points at a different size requires a second `addGlyphs()` call. This results in exactly two GPU draw calls (one for unselected, one for selected) regardless of the selection count, with no shader changes needed.

6. **Single dataset per plot**: Each ScatterPlotWidget displays a single X/Y data source pair.
   Users can open multiple scatter plot instances via the Analysis Dashboard for side-by-side
   comparison of different variable pairs.

# See Also

- [ScatterPlotWidget Documentation](index.qmd) — Full architecture and feature documentation
- [Plot Widget Guide](../plot_widget_guide.md) — Shared architecture, axis types, DataManager integration
- [LinePlotWidget Roadmap](../LinePlotWidget/roadmap.qmd) — Group integration pattern (Phase 1), feature coloring (Phase 3)
- [EventPlotWidget Roadmap](../EventPlotWidget/roadmap.qmd) — TensorData feature coloring/sorting (Phase 5)
- [HeatmapWidget Roadmap](../HeatmapWidget/roadmap.qmd) — Colormap infrastructure (Phase 2)
- [TemporalProjectionViewWidget](../TemporalProjectionViewWidget/index.qmd) — Polygon selection with PointData, shared rendering
